articles.title: Crash Closes Heywood Old Road in Middleton
articles.excerpt: Emergency crews are responding to a crash on Heywood Old Road in Middleton, which has resulted in the road being closed. Drivers are advised to avoid the area.
articles.content_html:
A crash has occurred on Heywood Old Road in Middleton, prompting emergency crews to respond to the scene. The road is currently closed due to the incident.

Motorists are advised to avoid the area while crews manage the situation. No further details about the crash have been released.

Authorities have not provided information regarding any injuries or the cause of the crash. Updates will follow as more information becomes available.

Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article.

articles.title: Two Men Arrested Following Investigation into Robbery and Thefts
articles.excerpt: A man from Middleton and another from Boise have been arrested following an investigation into a robbery and multiple burglaries and thefts in Nampa.
articles.content_html:
A man from Middleton and another from Boise have been arrested following an investigation into a robbery and multiple burglaries and thefts. The arrests occurred after Nampa Dispatch received a call reporting a robbery that had just taken place on July 8, 2026, at approximately 2:04 p.m.

The investigation revealed connections between the robbery and several other incidents of burglary and theft in the area. Authorities have not disclosed further details regarding the suspects or the specific locations involved in the crimes.

The arrests are part of ongoing efforts by law enforcement to address rising crime rates in the region. Police are expected to continue their investigation to determine if additional charges will be filed against the suspects.

Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article.

articles.title: Community protest planned in Middleton town centre next month
articles.excerpt: Organisers have announced plans for a public protest in Middleton on 8 August, inviting residents to attend and share their views on issues including illegal immigration and community concerns.
articles.summary: A demonstration has been announced for Middleton Gardens on Saturday 8 August. Organisers say the event will feature guest speakers and provide an opportunity for people to express their views on immigration and related issues.
articles.content_html:
A public protest has been scheduled to take place in Middleton town centre next month.

Organisers have announced that the event will begin at 1pm on Saturday 8 August at Middleton Gardens and are encouraging residents who wish to attend to do so peacefully and respectfully.

The demonstration is being promoted as a protest against illegal immigration and is expected to include speeches from invited guests and community campaigners.

Event details announced

According to promotional material shared online, the gathering will take place at Middleton Gardens, with attendees invited to bring flags and take part in the demonstration.

Named speakers currently include Ashlea Simon, Jamie Thomason and PagUK Billy, with organisers indicating that additional speakers may be announced closer to the event.

The event is being organised by campaigner Jamie Thomason, who has invited members of the public wishing to speak to contact him directly.

Community response expected

Public demonstrations often attract a range of opinions and viewpoints from residents. Organisers say the event is intended to provide a platform for people to voice concerns and discuss issues they believe affect local communities.

As with all public gatherings, attendees are expected to comply with relevant laws and any conditions imposed by local authorities or police to ensure the safety of participants and the wider public.

Residents advised to plan ahead

People visiting Middleton town centre on the day of the protest are advised to be aware of the planned gathering, which may lead to increased footfall in and around Middleton Gardens during the afternoon.

This report is based on promotional material and public social media posts advertising the event. Rochdale Daily is reporting the planned gathering as a matter of public interest and does not endorse any political organisation, campaign group or viewpoint associated with the event.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article reports the existence of a planned public demonstration. The views expressed by organisers, speakers or attendees are their own. Attendance at any public event should be peaceful and in accordance with applicable laws and public-order requirements.

articles.title: Middleton councillors explore new measures to improve town-centre safety
articles.excerpt: Reform UK councillors in Middleton are considering a cycling dismount zone, a review of CCTV provision and wider community-safety measures aimed at making the town centre safer and more welcoming.
articles.summary: Councillors say they are gathering advice from highways, legal and community-safety officers before deciding whether new restrictions or improvements should be introduced in Middleton town centre.
articles.content_html:
Middleton councillors are exploring a range of measures designed to improve pedestrian safety and make the town centre a more welcoming place for residents, visitors and businesses.

Reform UK councillors elected in May said they have been examining whether a cycling dismount zone could be introduced in parts of Middleton town centre.

The proposal is intended to address concerns about anti-social behaviour involving bicycles while improving safety for people walking through busy areas.

Focus on practical and enforceable measures

Councillors said an important part of the work will be establishing whether any new restriction could be enforced effectively.

They are seeking advice from highways, legal and community-safety officers before any decision is made.

The group said it wants to ensure that any action introduced is practical, proportionate and capable of making a visible difference.

Review of CCTV provision

The councillors are also calling for a review of the town centre’s CCTV network.

They said residents should be able to understand how public-safety infrastructure is being used and whether it is helping to deter anti-social behaviour, support enforcement and improve confidence in the town centre.

The review could consider how cameras are monitored, how footage is used and whether the current network is delivering the intended outcomes.

Creating a welcoming town centre

The statement highlights concerns involving bicycles, e-bikes and e-scooters, while stressing the wider goal of creating a safe and positive environment for everyone.

Councillors said Middleton town centre should be a place where people feel comfortable spending time, shopping and supporting local businesses.

They are continuing to gather information before bringing forward any formal proposals.

Residents invited to contribute

Residents, businesses, pedestrians and cyclists are being invited to share constructive feedback as the work develops.

The councillors said local experiences will be important in shaping measures that balance pedestrian safety, accessibility and the needs of people travelling through the town centre.

This report is based on a public statement issued by Reform UK councillors in Rochdale borough regarding anti-social behaviour and safety in Middleton town centre. No final policy decision has yet been made.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article reports proposals and views expressed by elected councillors. No cycling dismount zone, CCTV change or other new restriction has yet been formally approved or introduced.
articles.right_to_reply: Rochdale Borough Council, Greater Manchester Police, Middleton residents, local businesses, cyclists and anyone materially affected by this report may submit a response or request a factual correction by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

articles.title: Two people die in crash on Highway 101 near Kingston
articles.excerpt: A 75-year-old woman and a 28-year-old man, both from Middleton, died following a collision involving a tractor-trailer and a small car on Highway 101.
articles.content_html:
A 75-year-old woman and a 28-year-old man, both from Middleton, died at the scene following a crash on Highway 101 near Kingston on Tuesday afternoon.

Police reported that a tractor-trailer hauling hay collided with a small car at the Kingston exit. The impact resulted in the deaths of both occupants of the car.

Emergency services attended the scene, but both were pronounced dead shortly after the incident. The identities of the deceased have not yet been released pending notification of their families.

An investigation into the circumstances of the crash is ongoing. Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article.

The Green Flag Award is a mark of excellence for parks and green spaces, ensuring they meet the needs of the community and provide a safe and welcoming environment for visitors.

articles.excerpt: Middleton Public Library's Bookmobile will visit Middleton High School today, offering free books and lunch to students during the summer meals program.
articles.content_html:
A bookmobile from Middleton Public Library will be at Middleton High School today, July 15, from 11 a.m. to 12:30 p.m. This visit coincides with the Middleton-Cross Plains Area School District's (MCPASD) free summer meals program.

All MCPASD students are invited to select free books to keep and enjoy a complimentary lunch during this time. No registration is required for participation in the event.

This initiative aims to encourage reading among students while providing them with nutritious meals during the summer months. The bookmobile will offer a variety of books suitable for different age groups.

The event represents a collaborative effort to support students' educational needs and wellbeing during the school break.

articles.title: Potholes on Rochdale-Oldham Border Cause Frustration for Local Councillor
articles.excerpt: Residents have raised concerns over potholes at a junction on the border of Rochdale and Oldham, leading to frustration from local councillor Dylan Williams.
articles.content_html:
Potholes at a major junction on the border of Oldham and Rochdale have prompted complaints from residents. Councillor Dylan Williams, who represents East Middleton at Rochdale town hall, has expressed frustration over the lack of action regarding the deteriorating road surfaces between the two boroughs.

The issue is located at the junction where Greengate meets Grimshaw Lane, near a 'Welcome to Rochdale' sign, on the route into Middleton. Councillor Williams stated that despite being responsible for highways in Rochdale, he cannot address the potholes as they are situated just over the border in Oldham.

Councillor Williams noted that the potholes were marked for repair in March but have not been addressed since. He described the impact on vehicles, saying, "The bus rattles like mad when they go over it and so do cars." He added that with Oldham council currently lacking a leader, there is no clear point of contact for complaints other than council officers.

In response, Oldham council acknowledged the concerns regarding the potholes and confirmed that their highways team has inspected the area. They stated that the site is being considered for future planned repair works, subject to priorities across the borough.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

A family event is anticipated on the penultimate day of Wimbledon as the Princess of Wales is expected to be present in the Royal Box for the ladies’ singles final. The match will feature Karolína Muchová and Lin Zhu, both vying for the prestigious title.

The Princess, who serves as the patron of the All England Lawn Tennis and Croquet Club, has a longstanding association with the tournament, often attending key matches during the event.

This year’s Wimbledon has drawn significant attention, and the presence of the Princess is likely to enhance the occasion, reflecting the royal family's support for British sporting events.

As the tournament progresses, fans and attendees are looking forward to an exciting final, with Muchová and Zhu both demonstrating impressive performances throughout the competition.

articles.title: 18-Year-Old Man Charged After High-Speed Chase in Middleton
articles.excerpt: An 18-year-old man faces charges following a high-speed police chase in Middleton that reached speeds of 130 miles per hour and ended in a crash.
articles.content_html:
An 18-year-old man is facing charges after police say he led officers on a high-speed chase in Middleton. The pursuit reportedly reached speeds of 130 miles per hour before ending in a crash.

The incident began when police attempted to stop the man, who allegedly failed to comply and accelerated away from officers. The chase concluded when the vehicle collided with another object, resulting in damage.

Authorities have not disclosed any injuries related to the crash. The man is expected to appear in court to face the charges stemming from the incident. Police have not released further details about the charges or the circumstances surrounding the chase.

Anyone with information can find Greater Manchester Police and Crimestoppers contact details at the end of this article.

articles.title: Middleton Big Dig uncovers evidence of medieval Tonge Hall
articles.excerpt: More than 350 people, including pupils from five local schools, took part in the Middleton Big Dig and uncovered evidence of activity dating from the medieval period.
articles.summary: The community archaeological dig uncovered medieval ridge-and-furrow features and structures believed to relate to an earlier hall that stood on the Tonge Hall site before the existing Tudor building.
articles.content_html:
Evidence of medieval farming and structures connected with an earlier version of Tonge Hall has been uncovered during a community archaeological dig in Middleton.

More than 350 people took part in the Middleton Big Dig, including pupils from five local schools.

Rochdale Borough Council said participants found evidence of activity spanning from the medieval period to the present day.

Medieval landscape discovered

Among the discoveries was evidence of ridge-and-furrow farming, a landscape feature created by historic ploughing methods widely used during the Middle Ages.

Archaeologists and volunteers also uncovered structures believed to relate to the original medieval hall that existed before the Tudor building now standing on the site.

The surviving building is known as Tonge Hall.

More than 350 people involved

The project involved residents, volunteers and children from five schools, giving young people the opportunity to take part in practical archaeology and learn more about Middleton’s history.

Pupils from St Peter’s RC Primary School were among those who visited the site and helped examine finds uncovered during the dig.

Photographs shared by the council show pupils working alongside members of the archaeological team and examining material recovered from the excavation area.

Finds to be cleaned and catalogued

The objects and other material discovered during the project will now be cleaned, recorded and catalogued.

Once that work has been completed, the finds are expected to be offered to Touchstones Rochdale for inclusion in its collection.

The dig formed part of the activity programme supporting the restoration of the Broadfield Park Slopes.

The wider project has received funding from the National Lottery Heritage Fund.

This report is based on a public update and photographs published by Rochdale Borough Council following the completion of the Middleton Big Dig.
articles.event_location: Tonge Hall and Broadfield Park Slopes, Middleton
articles.right_to_reply: Rochdale Borough Council, the Middleton Big Dig organisers, participating schools and anyone materially affected by this report may submit a response or request a factual correction by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.
